ddls:
CREATE TABLE "information_elements" (
  "ie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Information Element */,
  "ie_name" TEXT /* Name of the Information Element */,
  "presence" TEXT /* Presence indicator (Mandatory or Optional) */,
  "range" TEXT /* Range of values accepted for this IE */,
  "ie_type_reference" TEXT /* Type and reference as per protocol specifications */,
  "semantics_description" TEXT /* Description of the semantics of the IE */,
  "criticality" TEXT /* Criticality of the IE to network function */,
  "assigned_criticality" TEXT /* Assigned criticality level for operational management */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protocol to which this IE belongs */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the IE */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the IE */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the IE */,
  "usage_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes on the usage of the IE */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_information_elements_assigned_criticality FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_criticality") REFERENCES criticality_levels ("criticality_level"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_information_elements_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_information_elements_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_information_elements_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "criticality_levels" (
  "criticality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each criticality level */,
  "criticality_level" TEXT /* Name of the criticality level (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the impact of this criticality level */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the criticality level */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the criticality level */,
  "review_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the criticality level should be reviewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("criticality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_criticality_levels_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_criticality_levels_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, auditor) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "last_password_change" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last password change */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "ie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Information Element being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "device_info" TEXT /* Device information used for access */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_ie_id FOREIGN KEY ("ie_id") REFERENCES information_elements ("ie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol (e.g., NR, LTE) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol details */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the protocol information */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the protocol information */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the protocol (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_protocols_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_protocols_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
